name: Vegetable-Bean Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place beans in a pot with 2 quarts of water. Bring to boil, then remove from heat and let rest for 1 hour.
2. Add chopped onion, garlic, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
3. Chop potatoes into large chunks; do not peel. Slice carrots 1/2 inch and zucchini 1 inch thick, scrub them (do not peel). Slice leeks and cut beans into 1-inch pieces. Add to soup pot along with seasonings. Simmer for 1 hour longer.
4. Thinly slice cabbage and add to soup along with rice or spaghetti. Simmer another 20 minutes. If the soup is too thick, add more water.
5. Add tomato wedges for the last 10 minutes before serving.
Helpful Hints:
Kidney beans make a good rich broth, but any kind of bean may be used. Soup freezes well; make a large batch and freeze half for use on a busy day. Try using some chopped spinach instead of cabbage. By changing the vegetables and beans used, the soup can be different each time you make it. It is a meal in itself.
